quotes: more than ~15% of running body text in any section

relations.note: Specifies the concrete required-bold elements within the general hierarchy/limit rule.
Specifies concrete forbidden bold targets complementing the general per-section limit.
Both govern bold usage discipline; exclusivity adds a cross-device combination constraint.

quotes: Bold is for **hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration**. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.

rules.intent: Prevent overuse of bold that would dilute its signaling value for structure and key claims.
rules.rule_text: Bold exists to signal hierarchy and key takeaways, not for decoration. Per email section, bold at most: the heading(s), one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure.

quotes: Always bold: callout text per the callout specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence (first instance per section only).

rules.intent: Guarantee that structurally important content (callouts, key stats, brand emphasis) is consistently visually reinforced.
rules.rule_text: Always bold: callout text per the callout specs, statistical callout numbers, and the brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence — but only the first instance per section.

quotes: more than ~15% of running body text in any section
Never bold: entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, the generic name.

rules.intent: Keep bold usage restrained so it retains emphasis value and does not obscure the generic name or overwhelm body copy.
rules.rule_text: Never bold entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text in any section, prepositional filler, or the generic (non-brand) product name.

effect.reserved_for: chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup only
effect.subject: bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis combined on the same string

quotes: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup.
Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

rules.intent: Avoid visual over-emphasis that reduces legibility and dilutes hierarchy signaling.
rules.rule_text: Do not combine bold, ALL CAPS, and color emphasis on the same string, except in chart headlines and the campaign headline lockup. In general, pick at most two emphasis devices per element.

quotes: reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations
No underlining except real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — reserve italics for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.

rules.intent: Preserve underline as a reliable link affordance and prevent italics from being misused for general emphasis in patient-facing copy.
rules.rule_text: No underlining except on real hyperlinks. No italic for emphasis in patient copy — italics are reserved for study names, notes ("Note:"), and citations.
